danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based antagonists (Trouble Bruin, key masters, minions of King Dark Demon)
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities that deal damage on contact; signal threat to player progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Enlarged, distinctly-designed enemy sprites positioned as stage climax encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger threshold; progression gate requiring skill demonstration

danger_signals.visual_form: Spotlight indicator in screen corner that changes color as damage accumulates
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability state; visual warning of proximity to death/life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual void below playable space (though with bounce-back mechanic rather than instant death)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; damage and repositioning threat rather than instant elimination

safety_signals.visual_form: 17 distinct head designs (spinning fireballs, invincibility forms, speed enhancements, health restoration)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary or sustained advantage state; visual communication of capability enhancement
Restoration opportunity; safety mechanism to counter damage accumulation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable items providing additional life reserves
safety_signals.meaning: Extended safety buffer; reduces consequence of single failure

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs appearing in every stage alongside Headdy
safety_signals.meaning: World stability and companionship; visual reinforcement that protagonist is not isolated against threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Puppet character with detachable head; Western animation-inspired visual design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in all stages as the active player avatar; associated with support characters

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil puppet king; distinct boss sprite design indicating authority/hierarchy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transforms puppets of the world into his minions; visual conversion mechanic indicates faction expansion through corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Minions (Trouble Bruin, key masters) populate stages as his army; stage bosses represent his power structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Puppet-themed world inhabited by transformed enemies; King Dark Demon's minions occupying stage spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; bosses serve as gatekeepers of progression; world is actively hostile

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Head design changes on Headdy; distinct visual transformation indicating different capability state (fireballs, speed, invincibility, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay mechanics shift with power-up type; attack patterns change; traversal abilities expand

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spotlight health indicator in corner transitions through color states as damage accumulates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One additional hit results in life loss; increased risk of stage restart

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment transitions to boss arena; enlarged enemy sprite with distinctive design vs. standard minions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat mechanics intensify; multi-phase attack patterns vs. simple minion interactions; stage gating

environmental_storytelling.example: Support characters appear in every stage, providing visual narrative of world stability and Headdy's not being alone against invasion; reinforces faction dynamics through repeated ally presence

environmental_storytelling.technique: Visual hierarchy via sprite scale and detail
environmental_storytelling.example: Boss enemies are enlarged and more elaborately designed than common minions; indicates rank/threat level and establishes power structure of King Dark Demon's faction

environmental_storytelling.technique: Puppet aesthetic as world identity marker
environmental_storytelling.example: All characters are puppets in a puppet world; inspired by Western animation and Terry Gilliam's visual style; establishes fantastical, controlled-environment narrative where transformation/corruption is thematic
17 distinct head designs that alter Headdy's appearance and function; visual communication that protagonist gains capabilities through world exploration and item discovery
Bottomless pits that bounce player back rather than instant-kill; communicates world rules (forgiving gravity) distinct from other platform games; reinforces puppet/artificial-world aesthetic

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can jump, throw head in any direction, grab hooks and pull to platforms; standard puppet platformer abilities

Behavior altered by specific head type—fireballs spin around head (damage increase), invincibility head (temporary invulnerability), speed head (movement increase), etc.; visual transformation indicates functional change
Positioned as stage encounter; sprite design distinctive enough to indicate named enemy status vs. generic minions
Serve as stage bosses; larger/more complex sprite; blocking progression until defeated
Attack on contact; standard platformer enemy behavior; indicate King Dark Demon's faction presence in environment
Not directly encountered until likely final stage; his transformation power manifests through minion presence; absence from most stages establishes him as hidden threat organizing corruption
Appear in every stage; provide visual companionship and world stability signal; suggest they are observing or assisting Headdy's progress

notes: Dynamite Headdy communicates world-state primarily through visual transformation mechanics (head power-ups), spotlight health indicator, and sprite-based enemy hierarchy. The puppet aesthetic itself is a manifestation layer—the artificial, constructed nature of puppets mirrors the controlled corruption by King Dark Demon. The game distinguishes danger/safety through enemy scale (bosses vs. minions), power-up visual distinctiveness, and health visualization. Faction identity is conveyed implicitly: Headdy is supported by NPCs and gains diverse heads (versatility/hope), while King Dark Demon's faction expands through transformation of existing puppets (corruption/loss). Western animation-inspired design reinforces a storybook-like narrative where visual clarity communicates threat and capability. Regional changes (NA difficulty increase, dialogue/sprite changes) affected how urgency/danger might have been communicated, but core manifestation systems remain sprite and color-based.
